assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response acknowledges spot instance instability and mentions preemptions, though doesn't explicitly detail the interaction with drain process

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention that spot workloads are typically designed for interruption, making them inherently suitable for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response shows maxSurge: 1 without recommending higher values for spot pools due to fault tolerance

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response explicitly sets maxUnavailable: 0 and advises against voluntary disruptions, opposite of the expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking spot instance availability and having backup instance types configured

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides PDB configuration example and recommends using them for workload protection

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response doesn't provide gcloud commands for configuring surge settings specifically, only shows YAML configuration

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests the opposite - upgrading on-demand nodes first for stability, then spot pools
